source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure ‘Log_error_verbosity’ Database Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to ‘DEFAULT’ or Stricter

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure ‘Log_error_verbosity’ Database Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to ‘DEFAULT’ or Stricter
input_row.description: The log_error_verbosity flag controls the verbosity/details of messages logged. Valid values are: • TERSE • DEFAULT • VERBOSE TERSE excludes the logging of DETAIL, HINT, QUERY, and CONTEXT error information. VERBOSE output includes the SQLSTATE error code, source code file name, function name, and line number that generated the error. Ensure an appropriate value is set to 'DEFAULT' or stricter.
input_row.rationale: Auditing helps in troubleshooting operational problems and also permits forensic analysis. If log_error_verbosity is not set to the correct value, too many details or too few details may be logged. This flag should be configured with a value of 'DEFAULT' or stricter. This recommendation is applicable to PostgreSQL database instances. Impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page 3. Go to Configuration card 4. Under Database flags, check the value of log_error_verbosity flag is set to 'DEFAULT' or stricter. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Use the below command for every Cloud SQL PostgreSQL database instance to verify the value of log_error_verbosity gcloud sql instances describe [INSTANCE_NAME] --format=json | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_error_verbosity")|.value' In the output, database flags are listed under the settings as the collection databaseFlags.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the PostgreSQL instance for which you want to enable the database flag. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add a Database Flag, choose the flag log_error_verbosity from the drop-down menu and set appropriate value. 6. Click Save to save your changes. 7. Confirm your changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure the log_error_verbosity database flag for every Cloud SQL PosgreSQL database instance using the below command. gcloud sql instances patch INSTANCE_NAME --database-flags log_error_verbosity=<TERSE|DEFAULT|VERBOSE> Note : This command will overwrite all database flags previously set. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags you want set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("="). Default Value: By default log_error_verbosity is DEFAULT. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags 2. https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-logging.html#GUC-LOG- ERROR-VERBOSITY Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.
input_row.impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags 2. https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-logging.html#GUC-LOG- ERROR-VERBOSITY Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be fully verified using Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`) commands, which are specified in the audit field. The audit approach is Automated because:

- The Google Cloud SDK has methods to check the `log_error_verbosity` flag via the `gcloud sql instances describe` command.
- The audit explicitly mentions using GCP CLI commands to verify this setting, indicating that it’s a standard, queryable property of a GCP service.
- This does not require SSH access or visual inspection; it's specifically about checking a configurable database parameter.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_log_error_verbosity_set_correctly

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would:

1. Use the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`) CLI to execute the command:
   ```
   gcloud sql instances describe [INSTANCE_NAME] --format=json
   ```
   This retrieves all configurations for a specific Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance.

2. Utilize `jq` or similar JSON parsing tools to extract the `log_error_verbosity` value:
   ```
   | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_error_verbosity")|.value'
   ```

3. Validate that the `log_error_verbosity` is set to 'DEFAULT' or 'TERSE'. These values are "DEFAULT" or stricter as per the control.

4. Enumerate all PostgreSQL instances in the GCP account and regions, applying the above check to each instance.

5. Pass/Fail Criteria:
   - Pass: The `log_error_verbosity` is set to 'DEFAULT' or 'TERSE'.
   - Fail: Any instance with `log_error_verbosity` set to 'VERBOSE' or missing entirely.

By automating this check, the CSPM tool ensures that all PostgreSQL instances comply with the recommended verbosity settings, minimizing exposure to excessive log data that could inflate storage costs or inadvertently expose sensitive information.
step2_review:
---

### 1. **APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated:** The classification as Automated is correct. The control can be fully validated using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands, without requiring manual inspection.
- **GCP SDK/CLI Capability:** The `gcloud sql instances describe` command retrieves all instance settings, including database flags. The `settings.databaseFlags` array in the output contains the `log_error_verbosity` value if it is set.
- **Other Automation Options:** This check could also be implemented using the Cloud SQL Admin API (`instances.get`), but the CLI approach is valid and commonly used for compliance automation.

---

### 2. **TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8/10

- **Automation Details:** The steps described are technically correct for extracting and evaluating the `log_error_verbosity` flag.
- **gcloud/CLI Commands:** The `gcloud sql instances describe [INSTANCE_NAME] --format=json` command is accurate. Using `jq` to parse the output is a standard approach.
- **Coverage:** The description correctly notes the need to enumerate all PostgreSQL instances. However, the method for enumerating all instances (e.g., `gcloud sql instances list --filter="databaseVersion~POSTGRES"`) is not explicitly mentioned and should be.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** The logic is sound, but the control should clarify what happens if the flag is **not set** (i.e., missing from `databaseFlags`). According to GCP, if a flag is not set, the default PostgreSQL value applies, which is "DEFAULT". This should be explicitly stated.
- **Program Name:** The program name `gcp_sql_instance_log_error_verbosity_set_correctly` is descriptive but could be more specific (see Improvements).

---

### 3. **IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Enumerate All Instances:** Explicitly mention using `gcloud sql instances list --filter="databaseVersion~POSTGRES"` to enumerate all PostgreSQL instances.
- **Missing Flag Handling:** Clarify that if the `log_error_verbosity` flag is **absent**, it should be treated as "DEFAULT" (the PostgreSQL default). This is important for accurate compliance reporting.
- **Alternative API:** For large environments, consider using the [Cloud SQL Admin API](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/admin-api/rest/v1beta4/instances/get) for programmatic checks at scale.
- **Program Name:** Suggest a more precise name:  
  `gcp_sql_postgresql_instance_log_error_verbosity_minimum`
- **Edge Cases:** Consider multi-region or multi-project environments—ensure the automation can iterate across all relevant projects, not just the current context.
- **Output Formatting:** Recommend outputting instance name, project, and region alongside the flag value for clear reporting.

---

### 4. **CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is technically sound, leverages appropriate GCP tooling, and aligns with security best practices for automation.

---

### 5. **FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- **Modify** the automation details to:
  - Explicitly include enumeration of all PostgreSQL instances (with sample command).
  - Clarify that a missing `log_error_verbosity` flag is compliant (treated as "DEFAULT").
  - Update the program name to `gcp_sql_postgresql_instance_log_error_verbosity_minimum` for clarity and convention.
  - Optionally, mention that the Cloud SQL Admin API can be used as an alternative for large-scale automation.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section             | Review                                                                                   |
|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation | AGREE                                                                                    |
| Technical Accuracy  | 8/10                                                                                    |
| Improvements        | Clarify enumeration, missing flag handling, program name, edge cases, API alternative   |
| Confidence          | HIGH                                                                                    |
| Final Recommendation| Modify (as above)                                                                       |

---
